metadata.name: arcade-google
metadata.summary: Arcade tools for the entire google suite

functions.name: arcade_google.tools.calendar.create_event

params.name: "The datetime when the event starts in ISO 8601 format, e.g., '2024-12-31T15:30:00'."
"The datetime when the event ends in ISO 8601 format, e.g., '2024-12-31T17:30:00'."
"The ID of the calendar to create the event in, usually 'primary'."
"The list of attendee emails. Must be valid email addresses e.g., username@domain.com."
"A dictionary containing the created event details"

documentation: Create a new event/meeting/sync/meetup in the specified calendar.

functions.name: arcade_google.tools.calendar.list_events

params.name: "Filter by events that end on or after this datetime in ISO 8601 format, e.g., '2024-09-15T09:00:00'."
"Filter by events that start before this datetime in ISO 8601 format, e.g., '2024-09-16T17:00:00'."
"The ID of the calendar to list events from"
"The maximum number of events to return"
"A dictionary containing the list of events"

documentation:
List events from the specified calendar within the given datetime range.

min_end_datetime serves as the lower bound (exclusive) for an event's end time.
max_start_datetime serves as the upper bound (exclusive) for an event's start time.

For example:
If min_end_datetime is set to 2024-09-15T09:00:00 and max_start_datetime is set to 2024-09-16T17:00:00,
the function will return events that:
1. End after 09:00 on September 15, 2024 (exclusive)
2. Start before 17:00 on September 16, 2024 (exclusive)
This means an event starting at 08:00 on September 15 and ending at 10:00 on September 15 would be included,
but an event starting at 17:00 on September 16 would not be included.

functions.name: arcade_google.tools.calendar.update_event

params.name: "The ID of the event to update"
"The updated datetime that the event starts in ISO 8601 format, e.g., '2024-12-31T15:30:00'."
"The updated datetime that the event ends in ISO 8601 format, e.g., '2024-12-31T17:30:00'."
"The updated ID of the calendar containing the event."
"The list of attendee emails to add. Must be valid email addresses e.g., username@domain.com."
"The list of attendee emails to remove. Must be valid email addresses e.g., username@domain.com."
"Should attendees be notified of the update? (none, all, external_only)"
"A string containing the updated event details, including the event ID, update timestamp, and a link to view the updated event."

documentation:
Update an existing event in the specified calendar with the provided details.
Only the provided fields will be updated; others will remain unchanged.

`updated_start_datetime` and `updated_end_datetime` are independent and can be provided separately.

functions.name: arcade_google.tools.calendar.delete_event

params.name: "The ID of the event to delete"
"The ID of the calendar containing the event"
"Specifies which attendees to notify about the deletion"
"A string containing the deletion confirmation message"

documentation: Delete an event from Google Calendar.

functions.name: arcade_google.tools.utils.parse_datetime
documentation:
Parse a datetime string in ISO 8601 format and ensure it is timezone-aware.

Args:
    datetime_str (str): The datetime string to parse. Expected format: 'YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS'.
    time_zone (str): The timezone to apply if the datetime string is naive.

Returns:
    datetime: A timezone-aware datetime object.

Raises:
    ValueError: If the datetime string is not in the correct format.

functions.name: arcade_google.tools.utils.parse_email
documentation:
Parse email data and extract relevant information.

Args:
    email_data (Dict[str, Any]): Raw email data from Gmail API.

Returns:
    Optional[Dict[str, str]]: Parsed email details or None if parsing fails.

functions.name: arcade_google.tools.utils.parse_draft_email
documentation:
Parse draft email data and extract relevant information.

Args:
    draft_email_data (Dict[str, Any]): Raw draft email data from Gmail API.

Returns:
    Optional[Dict[str, str]]: Parsed draft email details or None if parsing fails.

functions.name: arcade_google.tools.utils._get_email_body
documentation:
Extract email body from payload.

Args:
    payload (Dict[str, Any]): Email payload data.

Returns:
    Optional[str]: Decoded email body or None if not found.

functions.name: arcade_google.tools.utils._clean_email_body
documentation:
Remove HTML tags and clean up email body text while preserving most content.

Args:
    body (str): The raw email body text.

Returns:
    str: Cleaned email body text.

functions.name: arcade_google.tools.utils._clean_text
documentation:
Clean up the text while preserving most content.

Args:
    text (str): The input text.

Returns:
    str: Cleaned text.

functions.name: arcade_google.tools.utils._update_datetime
documentation:
Update the datetime for a Google Calendar event.

Args:
    day (Day | None): The day of the event.
    time (TimeSlot | None): The time of the event.
    time_zone (str): The time zone of the event.

Returns:
    dict | None: The updated datetime for the event.

classes.name: arcade_google.tools.models.Corpora
documentation:
Bodies of items (files/documents) to which the query applies.
Prefer 'user' or 'drive' to 'allDrives' for efficiency.
By default, corpora is set to 'user'.
Google Drive Models and Enums

classes.name: arcade_google.tools.models.OrderBy

class_variables.name: arcade_google.tools.models.OrderBy.CREATED_TIME
documentation: When the file was created (ascending)

class_variables.name: arcade_google.tools.models.OrderBy.CREATED_TIME_DESC
documentation: When the file was created (descending)

class_variables.name: arcade_google.tools.models.OrderBy.FOLDER
documentation: The folder ID, sorted using alphabetical ordering (ascending)

class_variables.name: arcade_google.tools.models.OrderBy.FOLDER_DESC
documentation: The folder ID, sorted using alphabetical ordering (descending)

class_variables.name: arcade_google.tools.models.OrderBy.MODIFIED_TIME
documentation: The last time the file was modified by anyone (ascending)

class_variables.name: arcade_google.tools.models.OrderBy.NAME
documentation: The name of the file, sorted using alphabetical ordering (e.g., 1, 12, 2, 22) (ascending)

class_variables.name: arcade_google.tools.models.OrderBy.NAME_DESC
documentation: The name of the file, sorted using alphabetical ordering (e.g., 1, 12, 2, 22) (descending)

class_variables.name: arcade_google.tools.models.OrderBy.NAME_NATURAL
documentation: The name of the file, sorted using natural sort ordering (e.g., 1, 2, 12, 22) (ascending)

class_variables.name: arcade_google.tools.models.OrderBy.NAME_NATURAL_DESC
documentation: The name of the file, sorted using natural sort ordering (e.g., 1, 2, 12, 22) (descending)

class_variables.name: arcade_google.tools.models.OrderBy.RECENCY
documentation: The most recent timestamp from the file's date-time fields (ascending)

class_variables.name: arcade_google.tools.models.OrderBy.SHARED_WITH_ME_TIME_DESC
documentation: When the file was shared with the user, if applicable (descending)

class_variables.name: arcade_google.tools.models.OrderBy.STARRED
documentation: Whether the user has starred the file (ascending)

class_variables.name: arcade_google.tools.models.OrderBy.STARRED_DESC
documentation: Whether the user has starred the file (descending)

Sort keys for ordering files in Google Drive.
Each key has both ascending and descending options.

functions.name: arcade_google.tools.drive.list_documents

params.name: "Keywords or phrases that must be in the document title"
"Sort order. Defaults to listing the most recently modified documents first"
"Whether the requesting application supports both My Drives and shared drives"
"A dictionary containing 'documents_count' (number of documents returned) and 'documents' (a list of document details including 'kind', 'mimeType', 'id', and 'name' for each document)"

documentation: List documents in the user's Google Drive. Excludes documents that are in the trash.
Implements: https://googleapis.github.io/google-api-python-client/docs/dyn/drive_v3.files.html#list
Example `arcade chat` query: `list my 5 most recently modified documents`
TODO: Support query with natural language. Currently, the tool expects a fully formed query string as input with the syntax defined here: https://developers.google.com/drive/api/guides/search-files

functions.name: arcade_google.tools.docs.get_document_by_id

params.name: "The ID of the document to retrieve."

documentation: Get the latest version of the specified Google Docs document.
Uses https://developers.google.com/docs/api/reference/rest/v1/documents/get
Example `arcade chat` query: `get document with ID 1234567890`
Note: Document IDs are returned in the response of the Google Drive's `list_documents` tool

functions.name: arcade_google.tools.docs.insert_text_at_end_of_document

params.name: "The ID of the document to update."
"The text content to insert into the document"
"The response from the batchUpdate API as a dict."

documentation: Updates an existing Google Docs document using the batchUpdate API endpoint.
Uses https://developers.google.com/docs/api/reference/rest/v1/documents/batchUpdate
Example `arcade chat` query: `insert "The END" at the end of document with ID 1234567890`

functions.name: arcade_google.tools.docs.create_blank_document

params.name: "The title of the blank document to create"
"The created document's title, documentId, and documentUrl in a dictionary"

documentation: Create a blank Google Docs document with the specified title.
Uses https://developers.google.com/docs/api/reference/rest/v1/documents/create
Example `arcade chat` query: `create blank document with title "My New Document"`

functions.name: arcade_google.tools.docs.create_document_from_text

params.name: "The title of the document to create"
"The text content to insert into the document"
"The created document's title, documentId, and documentUrl in a dictionary"

documentation: Create a Google Docs document with the specified title and text content.
Uses https://developers.google.com/docs/api/reference/rest/v1/documents/batchUpdate
Example `arcade chat` query: `create document with title "My New Document" and text content "Hello, World!"`
